codeReview.summary: The reviewed commit is correctly scoped to proof-only test work. It replaces the earlier struct stand-ins with actual Rust enums in the plain Store regression and adds a trybuild compile-fail fixture that preserves the unsupported direct secure-enum boundary. The claimed boundary proof is valid for this feature, while the real enum roundtrip proof gap noted in the earlier failed follow-up review is now closed in HEAD by this commit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill was updated after the earlier failed enum-proof follow-up to require commit-surface confirmation in verification-only sessions, and this worker followed that stronger rule by touching the promised enum test surface directly. No additional shared-state gap was evident in this feature review.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The feature commit 8e121b0cb444b506422c7cd2d86fd98034bba728 modifies core/tests/integration_db.rs, core/tests/sensitive_compile.rs, and the new UI fixture files, matching the skill guidance in .factory/skills/rust-library-worker/SKILL.md step 9.

summary: Pass. Commit 8e121b0 adds the actual-Rust-enum plain Store regression plus the direct secure-enum compile-fail fixture, so the feature now validly preserves the secure-enum boundary and provides the intended initial m3 groundwork.
